title: SignalScout — AI-powered automation for prediction markets like Kalshi and Polymarket
author: cherry-pick-crew
selftext:
Prediction markets are a fascinating space for AI-assisted trading. Contracts resolve on verifiable real-world events, prices reflect crowd probability estimates, and the markets are still inefficient enough that systematic approaches have a real edge.

I built **SignalScout** to make automated trading on these platforms accessible without an engineering background. Price alerts, automated execution, and cross-platform market discovery across Kalshi and Polymarket.

The backend infrastructure (AgentBase at [https://www.useagentbase.dev/](https://www.useagentbase.dev/)) is built for developers and teams who want to go deeper — strategy hosting, execution engines, market monitoring at scale.

For anyone exploring AI-driven approaches to event contract trading, would love to connect.

🍎 App Store: [https://apps.apple.com/us/app/signalscout-eventmarketalerts/id6759851620](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/signalscout-eventmarketalerts/id6759851620) 🤖 Android beta: DM me your email to get added   
📡 Discord + more: [https://linktr.ee/signalscoutapp](https://linktr.ee/signalscoutapp)

comments.author: Patient_Kangaroo4864
comments.body: “AI edge” in prediction markets usually gets eaten by fees, slippage, and thin liquidity pretty fast. If you’re not beating those consistently, automation just scales losses.

replies.author: cherry-pick-crew
replies.body: It really depends on what you use AI for. There is enough uncertainty in markets with meaningful volume that you can still use automation assisted by AI for position sizing, alerts or simply taking action on your behalf so that you’re not constantly monitoring feeds.
